reasons_to_visit.details: April brings cherry, magnolia, and tulip displays at the Botanical Gardens, Parc des Eaux-Vives, Parc La Grange, and Parc des Bastions — the lakefront feels reborn after winter.
Geneva's flagship book fair at Palexpo (typically late March or April) brings authors, readers, and literary programming from across the Francophonie — a relaxed, well-attended cultural anchor.
CGN's full spring schedule kicks in, including the historic belle-époque paddle steamers running to Nyon, Lausanne, and Yvoire — one of Europe's great affordable scenic day trips.
Average highs of 13–17°C and long evenings make April excellent for walking the Old Town, climbing the cathedral towers, and looping the lake bridges.
Higher resorts (Chamonix, Verbier, Zermatt) ski strong into mid-April, offering uncrowded slopes, soft snow, and lunch on a sunny terrace.
Outside Easter and major Palexpo events, April is one of the year's most pleasant low-cost windows in the city.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Atlantic systems regularly produce rain bursts and afternoon thunderstorms; an umbrella is essential.
Good Friday and Easter Monday are full public holidays — most shops close and many small restaurants take long weekends.
April is peak season for birch and other tree pollens. Sensitive travellers should bring antihistamines.
Lake Geneva is still in the low teens — swimming remains a brave undertaking. Bains des Pâquis is reopening but not yet summer-busy.
Above 1,500m, trails in the Jura and Salève can still be patchy with snow and mud; consult local trail reports.
UN agencies and watch industry events resume in earnest, lifting weekday hotel demand around Place des Nations and Pâquis.

weather.precipitation: Variable: glorious sunshine alternating with cool showers. Long evenings extend to 20:30 by mid-month.
weather.general_tips: Pack layers, light rain shell, and sunglasses. First T-shirt afternoons on lakefront terraces happen here.

tourism_level.crowds: Building, especially on Easter weekend and during Salon du Livre.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week; weekend rates climb. Easter weekend spikes if it falls in this window.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Spring optimism. Terraces full at the first hint of sun; runners line the quais.

events_holidays.name: Easter Holidays (when in April)
events_holidays.description: Good Friday and Easter Monday are full public holidays — banks, offices, and most shops closed; museums and lake cruises typically run.
events_holidays.notes: Book restaurants for Easter Sunday brunch early. Many lakeside hotels run Easter packages.

events_holidays.name: Salon du Livre Geneva
events_holidays.description: Major Francophone book fair at Palexpo: authors in conversation, themed pavilions, children's literature programming.
events_holidays.notes: Day tickets affordable. Take Tram 18 or train to Palexpo.

events_holidays.name: Spring Programme at Victoria Hall
events_holidays.description: Orchestre de la Suisse Romande and visiting ensembles fill Victoria Hall with major spring concerts.
events_holidays.notes: Best acoustics in central Geneva. Reduced-price last-minute student tickets sometimes available.

unique_experiences.description: Climb the north tower for one of Geneva's best panoramas — the lake stretching to Lausanne, Mont Blanc to the south, and the Jura to the west.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tower entry is small fee separate from cathedral entry. Combine with the Site Archéologique beneath the cathedral.

unique_experiences.where: Palexpo (Tram 18 or train one stop from Cornavin)
unique_experiences.description: Browse pavilions of Francophone publishers, sit in on author conversations, and grab a glass of Genevan Chasselas at the wine bar.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free wardrobes; lunch options on-site fine but pricey.

unique_experiences.description: CGN's restored belle-époque paddle steamers (Savoie, Simplon, Italie) start spring runs in April. Ride 45 minutes along the south shore to the lake-castle town of Nyon for lunch and a Roman museum.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Swiss Travel Pass covers regular routes; first-class supplement is small and includes a calmer top deck.

unique_experiences.where: Conservatoire et Jardin botaniques (Tram 15 to Jardin Botanique)
unique_experiences.description: Free 28-hectare garden with magnolia avenue, alpine rockeries, and protected glasshouses — one of Geneva's most underrated spring assets.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Allow 1.5–2 hours. Lakefront café at the entrance.

weather.precipitation: Generally drier and warmer than early month. Thunderstorms more likely later in the period.
weather.general_tips: Excellent walking and cycling conditions. Sunscreen on clear days; UV climbs quickly.

tourism_level.crowds: Healthy mid-week, busy on weekends. Easter weekend (if late April) very busy.
tourism_level.pricing: Mid-range; lakefront properties book ahead for weekends.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Full spring momentum: cafés full, runners and cyclists out, weekend day trips to Lavaux and Yvoire popular.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Half-Marathon (variable date)
events_holidays.description: Major running event around the lakefront and Old Town, drawing thousands of runners and closing the quais.
events_holidays.notes: Spectacular spectator atmosphere; expect tram and traffic detours.

events_holidays.name: Spring Vintage at Domaine de Châteauvieux
events_holidays.description: Several Geneva-area Michelin-starred restaurants debut spring tasting menus.
events_holidays.notes: Reserve two to four weeks ahead for weekend dinners.

unique_experiences.activity: Cycle the Voie Verte to Annemasse
unique_experiences.where: Eaux-Vives → Annemasse via the Voie Verte greenway
unique_experiences.description: Cross-border bike path along the old railway corridor, with cafés, art murals, and easy connections back to Geneva via Léman Express.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Donkey Republic and Genève Roule rent bikes from Place de Montbrillant.

unique_experiences.activity: Maison Tavel + Old Town Architecture Walk
unique_experiences.description: Geneva's oldest private home houses an excellent city museum, including the famed 1850 Magnin relief map. Combine with a circuit of the Old Town's hidden courtyards.
Bigger spring vegetable and flower selection arrives by mid-April. Linger for an apéro on a terrace afterwards.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12. Wednesday and Saturday morning markets best.

unique_experiences.description: Tour the Science Gateway exhibitions and, if available, book a guided visit deeper into CERN itself.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Science Gateway free, no reservation for most exhibits. Guided tours book out weeks in advance.
